title: Phaistos Disc Statistical Analysis Workbench
description: An interactive, browser-based statistical analysis workbench for the Phaistos Disc. Implements a single Monte Carlo framework (100,000 iterations per language; seeded mulberry32 PRNG; uniform and frequency-weighted nulls) for testing candidate phonetic readings against nine Bronze Age language hypotheses (Minoan/Linear A, Mycenaean Greek, Luwian, West Semitic, Proto-Finno-Ugric, Middle Egyptian, Hurrian, Etruscan, Hittite via Rasmussen's independent sign values). Includes sensitivity analyses (sign 02 determinative vs phonetic; sign 18 TI vs RJU; allograph merge), two circularity controls (uniform reshuffling and frequency-weighted reshuffling), a leave-PD-inspired-out ablation, a 28-card extended-analysis battery (entropy, Bayes factors, positional structure, Davis-style replication, etc.), and continuous-integration gates (verify-bundle, verify-canonical, verify-prose, verify-engine-invariants, verify-framing, plus engine math unit tests) that enforce consistency between the engine output and every numerical claim made in the accompanying manuscript. Companion paper: A Reproducible Multi-Language Statistical Test of Phonetic Readings for the Phaistos Disc (Shandrokha 2026).
_comment_preprint: Once the arXiv preprint has a DOI, append to related_identifiers: { "identifier": "10.48550/arXiv.<NNNN.NNNNN>", "relation": "isSupplementTo", "resource_type": "publication-preprint" }.
_comment_journal: Once the journal version is in press or published, append: { "identifier": "<journal DOI>", "relation": "isSupplementTo", "resource_type": "publication-article" }.
